name: May I?
description: Human approvals for agent actions via May I? (mayi.sh). Install it, click Connect on the agent's Deployment tab (Eden registers the OAuth client automatically — no operator app to create), and gated tools park until a human approves in the May I? app, then resume with a signed receipt. Scheduled work that may need an approval must use the handler form of an eve schedule and hand the run to the channel with receive(mayi, ...) — task-mode schedules can't park for a human.

secrets.name: MAYI_CALLBACK_STATE_KEY
secrets.description: Stable base64url 32-byte key the adapter encrypts callback state with — minted once by Eden at first deploy and kept identical across redeploys (rotating it would strand outstanding approvals).

secrets.name: MAYI_CALLBACK_STATE_KEY_ID
secrets.description: The current callback-state key's rotation identifier (a constant "k1") — provisioned automatically by Eden at deploy alongside the key.

setup:
**Connect:** after installing, open the agent's **Deployment** tab and click **Connect May I?** on the Connections card. Eden registers a dedicated OAuth client with May I? automatically — covering the exact approval-callback URL of every environment the agent has — then walks you through one PKCE consent screen. Nothing to copy, no OAuth app to create.

**Self-host note (operators):** set `EDEN_PUBLIC_ORIGIN` to Eden's **publicly reachable HTTPS origin** before connecting. May I? validates every callback URL as public HTTPS at registration time, and the instance's approval callbacks arrive at `<EDEN_PUBLIC_ORIGIN>/e/<environmentId>/eve/v1/mayi/approval-resolved` — a local-dev host can't receive them (Connect will surface a readable registration error on localhost).

**Adding an environment later:** May I? OAuth clients are immutable with exact-match callback URLs, so an environment created after you connect can't receive approval callbacks until you **Reconnect** (the Connections card flips to "reconnect needed" and one reconnect registers a fresh client covering the new set).

**How credentials flow:** May I? rotates the OAuth refresh token on every refresh, so the instance never holds it. Eden keeps the grant, refreshes centrally, and the shipped `credentials.server.ts` fetches short-lived access tokens from Eden's token broker (about one call per hour). Approval verification keys come from May I?'s public JWKS at runtime — never injected.

**eve version:** `@mayiapp/eve` pins eve `0.24.2`; keep the agent on that version until the adapter advances its peer range.
